title: inula britannica var. japonica
intro: Inula britannica var. japonica, also known as Japanese elecampane, is a variety of the elecampane plant, a flowering perennial native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Japanese elecampane include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to support immune function and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the roots of the Japanese elecampane plant are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
